mnemonics: Send a KILL signal to RAISE an alarm in a process.
keyPoints: kill() sends a signal to a process or a group of processes.
raise() sends a signal to the current process itself.
Signals are a method of inter-process communication (IPC) for notifying a process of an event.
commonMistakes: Assuming kill() always terminates the target process (it only sends a signal, which can be caught or ignored).
Confusing the process ID argument for kill(); using 0, negative, or -1 has special meanings for process groups.
